title.purpose: Card titles, section headers, setting group labels.

label.purpose: Buttons, navigation labels, list item titles.

shadows.name: navigation
shadows.purpose: Separates bottom navigation from scrolling content.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Lifts dialogs and bottom sheets above the page.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main action on any screen — start monitoring, stop alarm, install update.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #c43e2c; color: #ffffff; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; line-height: 1.25; padding: 16px 24px; height: 56px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #a83222; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0077a3; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Lower-emphasis actions on cards and settings.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #f5f4f3; color: #1c1917; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; line-height: 1.25; padding: 12px 20px; height: 48px; border: 1px solid #e7e5e3; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #ffffff; border-color: #c43e2c; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0077a3; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Groups related information or controls with generous internal padding.
components.html:
Alarm

Alarm is on and monitoring FreeStyle Libre notifications.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #f5f4f3; color: #1c1917; border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.35; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; color: #3d3a38; }

components.name: Switch
components.description: Large toggle for enabling monitoring, alarm, or settings.
components.css: .ds-switch { width: 52px; height: 32px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #c43e2c; border: none; padding: 4px; cursor: pointer; position: relative; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-switch[aria-checked='false'] { background: #e7e5e3; } .ds-switch-thumb { display: block; width: 24px; height: 24px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #ffffff; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(28, 25, 23, 0.15); transform: translateX(20px); transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-switch[aria-checked='false'] .ds-switch-thumb { transform: translateX(0); } .ds-switch:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0077a3; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Low Glucose Badge
components.description: High-contrast status indicator for low glucose events.
components.css: .ds-badge-low { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #b52b1f; color: #ffffff; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1.35; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px; }

components.name: High Glucose Badge
components.description: Amber status indicator for high glucose events; uses dark text for contrast.
components.css: .ds-badge-high { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #c49600; color: #1c1917; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1.35; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 4px; }

components.name: Bottom Navigation
components.description: Three-item bottom bar for Home, History, and Settings.
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; justify-content: space-around; align-items: center; height: 64px; background: #f5f4f3; border-top: 1px solid #e7e5e3; padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom); } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 4px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: #6b6866; text-decoration: none; padding: 8px 16px; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-nav-item-active { color: #c43e2c; } .ds-nav-item:hover { color: #1c1917; }

components.name: Alarm Screen
components.description: Full-screen alarm with one primary dismiss action and three snooze options.
components.html:
Glucose Alert

Snooze for:
components.css: .ds-alarm { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 100vh; padding: 32px; background: #c43e2c; color: #ffffff; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; text-align: center; } .ds-alarm-icon { margin-bottom: 24px; } .ds-alarm-title { font-size: 36px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.15; letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin: 0 0 32px; } .ds-alarm-dismiss { width: 100%; max-width: 400px; height: 56px; background: #ffffff; color: #c43e2c; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; border: none; border-radius: 8px; margin-bottom: 24px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-alarm-snooze-label { font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0 0 12px; opacity: 0.9; } .ds-alarm-snoozes { display: flex; gap: 8px; width: 100%; max-width: 400px; } .ds-alarm-snoozes button { flex: 1; height: 48px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12); color: #ffffff; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; }

narrative.overview: This is the visual system for an alarm companion that sits next to a person while they sleep. The interface is calm, human, and quietly confident during the day, then transforms into something impossible to miss at night. Every choice answers one question: can someone half-asleep, under stress, understand what is happening in a single glance? The system rejects clinical coldness, fitness-app playfulness, and marketing-page theatricality. It borrows clarity from Apple Health — clean type, generous spacing, clear states — but softens the edges so the app feels like it was made for a loved one, not issued by a hospital.
keyCharacteristics: One humanist sans (Inter) for every role. No display/body pairing; consistency is the voice.
Restrained color strategy: pure white floor, one warm-red primary for alarms and primary actions, one cool-blue accent for information and links.
Large, friendly touch targets. The alarm dismiss button is 56px high and full-width.
Flat, tonal layering. Depth comes from surface color, not shadows.
Day and night modes are first-class. Dark mode is near-black with warm-tinted neutrals to keep it from feeling cold.

rules.name: The One Alarm Color Rule
rules.body: Warm red is the only color that screams. Use it for the alarm screen, stop-alarm actions, and primary CTAs. Never use it for decoration, badges, or inactive states.

rules.name: The Pure Floor Rule
rules.body: The background is pure white in light mode and near-black in dark mode. Warmth lives in the brand colors and typography, not in a tinted surface.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Inter carries every text role. Weight and size create hierarchy; font switching creates noise.

rules.name: The 56dp Alarm Rule
rules.body: The alarm screen uses Display at a size that is readable from arm's length in a dark room. Nothing else competes with it.

rules.name: The Tonal-First Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. If you think you need a shadow, try a surface color change first.

dos: Use #c43e2c only for alarms, stop-alarm actions, and primary CTAs. Its scarcity is the point.
Keep every touch target at or above 48dp; alarm actions are 56dp.
Use Inter for every text role. Weight and size create hierarchy.
Respect dark mode as a first-class surface. The alarm screen still uses warm red; text and cards shift to warm-tinted dark neutrals.
Use color-independent indicators alongside color: a warning icon with a low badge, text labels with status dots.
Keep the alarm screen minimal: one headline, one dismiss button, three snooze options. Nothing else.
donts: Use cream, sand, beige, or warm-tinted backgrounds. The floor is pure white in light mode and near-black in dark mode. Warmth comes from the alarm red, not the canvas.
Use a separate display font or decorative serif. PRODUCT.md rejects AI/SaaS marketing tropes like slab-serif editorial type; this app is a tool, not a magazine.
Use coral, purple gradients, neon accents, glassmorphism, or gradient text. The existing code copied these from Claude.com; they are prohibited here.
Use heavy shadows or card grids as decoration. Cards group information; they are not a visual motif.
Put a tiny uppercase eyebrow above every section. One screen, one clear heading.
Use side-stripe borders greater than 1px as colored accents on cards or alerts.
Rely on color alone to distinguish high from low glucose. Pair the badge color with a text label and, where possible, an icon.
